page: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv7 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The instance features 64 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes a large 480 MB L3 cache, alongside 48 KB L1d and 2 MB L2 caches. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs.

Benchmark data highlights strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieved a top-tier Passmark CPU score of 87,182.31 and a strong multi-core stress-ng CPU performance of 68,669.54. Single-threaded performance is also notable, with top-tier results in gzip compression at 35.77 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.77 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 5,133.04 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 326.02 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. For database operations, the instance scored in the top tier on Passmark database operations at 32,324.96 and demonstrated strong performance in Redis SET operations at 113.0 million operations per second.

The primary tradeoff of the D64ls_v7 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core and the complete absence of local storage, requiring external network-attached storage for data persistence. However, the high-density compute configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for CPU-bound workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. It is highly suitable for compute-intensive tasks such as static web serving, where it achieved a top-tier throughput of 89.08 GB/sec, as well as multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is a compute-optimized server from the Dlsv7 family, featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz, this x86_64 architecture instance includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. The server does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on external Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate top-tier performance in static web serving, database operations, and single-threaded compression, alongside strong multi-core CPU capabilities and high memory bandwidth. With a low memory-to-CPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, the D64ls_v7 represents a cost-efficient choice for compute-heavy, memory-light workloads. It is highly suited for high-throughput web serving, multi-threaded data decompression, database operations, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is a compute-optimized server with 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier performance in web serving and database operations.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ls_v7 is a compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier web serving performance.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-dense virtual machine designed for intensive multi-threaded processing, database operations, and high-throughput web serving without local storage.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz with 64 dedicated vCPUs
Features 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a compute-optimized 2.0 GB per core ratio
Delivers top-tier performance in static web serving and database operations
Includes advanced hardware acceleration flags such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX
Lacks local storage and GPUs, requiring external storage solutions for data persistence
Best suited for compute-bound workloads, multi-threaded decompression, and web serving
